patterns.label: Eval Enablement Deck Proliferation
patterns.llmRationale: Multiple team members independently authoring separate eval decks (customer intro, pitch, enablement, summit) with explicit team confusion about which version is canonical. High SGI signal: Standardization Gap Index risk present from first cycle. Parallel Creation Gap archetype. Automation target: canonical eval deck generator replacing 3+ parallel versions.

patterns.label: Loop PM Review Meeting Notes Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring Loop-based meeting notes for PM reviews (e.g. MCS Analytics/Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review) with consistent structure: agenda, decisions, open questions. High automation ceiling — Loop is default format, template structure is well-established. Chain head for downstream action item tracking. Cross-source: meeting content lands in Loop docs, then referenced in email threads.

patterns.label: Eval Coaching and Scoping Bottleneck
patterns.llmRationale: High-frequency inbound eval guidance requests (8 internal + 4 customer deep-dive per cycle, 11.33 hrs). Covers 10+ request types: method selection, test case count, pass rate thresholds, eval-driven development timing, customer sanity checks, tool debugging, live demos. Expert Scaling Bottleneck archetype — single DRI absorbs all requests. Force-multiplier: unblocking this frees the entire eval ecosystem.

patterns.label: Email-to-Meeting-to-Shared-Doc Workflow Chain
patterns.llmRationale: Most commonly identified cross-source workflow: email with doc/idea triggers meeting, meeting outcomes land in Loop doc. Appears across PM reviews, eval work, and cross-team coordination. Named pipeline candidate (Ambiguous-to-Artifact archetype). Cross-source scoring bonus applies. Automating handoffs reduces context loss at each stage transition.

patterns.label: Compliance and Security Alert Handling
patterns.llmRationale: Microsoft Purview file-blocked alerts with consistent subject pattern and no-reply sender. Machine-parseable, deterministic, action-required format. High automation ceiling: classify alert type, assess file sensitivity, recommend action (acknowledge/escalate/unblock). Interruption cost far exceeds actual resolution complexity.

patterns.label: SDR Discovery Documentation Format Duplication
patterns.llmRationale: Different team members independently creating Word SDRs, PowerPoint discovery decks, and simplified templates. Explicit team discussion about whether a single format should exist. Standardization Gap Index risk. Automation target: canonical SDR template generator with meeting-to-SDR conversion workflow. Parallel Creation Gap archetype.

patterns.label: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Dominant email category this cycle (30 signals, 199 total emails mentioned). ADO Tasks, Bugs, Requirements, Epics, Features — all parseable by notification type. Low per-email cognitive load but massive cumulative scan time (22.5 hrs). Prime decomposition candidate into sub-classifiers. Notification Triage archetype with highest automationScore in current portfolio.

patterns.label: Recurring Weekly Meeting Cadence
patterns.llmRationale: Nine distinct recurring weekly meetings identified (1:1, Project Crucible, CAPE Architecture, US Pod Standup, Customer Engagement Sync, BizChat Office Hours, Deploy Agents Office Hours, Learning Fridays, MCS PM Review). All have fixed time, fixed attendees, and fixed agenda type. Strong pre-meeting prep automation target: pull context, draft agenda, surface open items.

patterns.label: JSON Config and Agent State File Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: Most frequently appearing file type this cycle (20 occurrences, 30 hrs). Configuration/state files, task artifacts, session/workspace/debug outputs. Machine-readable format with deterministic structure. Automation target: JSON config scaffolding from natural language spec, plus state-file-to-summary translation. High auto score offset by single-participant lower value.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact and Repo Sharing
patterns.llmRationale: High-frequency cross-chat sharing of GitHub repos, PowerPoint decks, Loop pages, aka.ms links, npm packages, YAML files, and zips. Distributed across many different chats rather than a single thread. Automation target: curated artifact registry with contextual sharing assistant. Precursor to skill-library socialization pattern.

patterns.label: Product Feedback Routing and VoC Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Repeated questions about where eval feedback goes, who owns ADO epics, how VoC reaches the product group, and what cadence exists for review. Ownership ambiguity between PM, TPM, and field. Automation target: routing guide generator plus ADO ticket scaffolder from VoC input.

patterns.label: Daily Planning Page Creation
patterns.llmRationale: Date-stamped daily planning pages ('[Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day') created with M365 Copilot as reusable template. Frequency 5/week, single-participant, consistent structure. This is the inaugural detection of Archetype 27: Personal Work Ritual. Automation target: context-aware daily briefing generator pulling calendar, flagged emails, and priority tasks. 15-20 min/day = 60-80 hrs/yr compounding savings.

patterns.label: Eval Tool Bug and Behavior Debugging
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring requests to diagnose confusing eval behavior: CSV export/import losing settings, adaptive cards not evaluating correctly, inconsistent results across runs, grader misconfiguration. Known failure modes are automatable. Skill would provide decision tree: symptom -> root cause -> fix steps. Feeds into eval-coaching-bottleneck pattern.

patterns.label: External Customer Follow-Up Email Loop
patterns.llmRationale: External customer threads with multi-day cadence: meeting -> follow-up email -> responses with attachments. Meets Customer-Signal-to-Toolkit named pipeline criteria. Automation target: post-meeting follow-up email drafter with key decisions, next steps, and attachment references included.

patterns.label: EvalCon Planning and Content Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: EvalCon planning appears repeatedly: what CAPE should present, framing content around blockers/challenges, submission deadlines, v-team coordination. Deadline-amplified (EvalCon 2026-04-02 = 13 days away). Event/Program Coordination archetype. Automation target: event submission assistant that frames team narrative, tracks deadlines, and coordinates content contributions.

patterns.label: CAPE Camp AIR Multi-Day Immersion Program
patterns.llmRationale: Full-day Camp AIR blocks (AI-first mindset, workflow analysis, agent creation, vibe coding, Power Apps, Dataverse, Copilot Studio). Largest contiguous time block this cycle (420 min). Immersion Cascade Detection applies: this single event will amplify 4-6 downstream patterns (apply 0.7x trend dampening to those patterns). Quarterly Immersion Cadence archetype. Automation target: post-immersion artifact packager.

candidateSkills.name: eval-deck-standardizer
candidateSkills.description: Generates a canonical eval enablement deck from structured inputs (customer tier, use case, maturity stage), replacing ad-hoc parallel creation by multiple team members. Supports customer intro, pitch, and summit deck variants from a single canonical template.
triggerExamples: We have four different eval decks floating — can you create one standard version?
I need a customer-facing eval intro deck for tomorrow's call
The field is confused about which eval deck to use — standardize it
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates eval deck proliferation (SGI risk, 3+ hrs/cycle of redundant creation) and ensures consistent messaging across PM, field, and customers. One canonical source replaces parallel creation debt and reduces customer-facing inconsistency.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers the 10 most common eval design questions on demand — method selection, test case count, pass rate thresholds, timing in agent lifecycle, grader configuration, and CLI troubleshooting — routing complex cases to the right DRI. Codifies the single-DRI bottleneck into a scalable, always-available skill.
triggerExamples: What eval method should I use for this declarative agent?
How many test cases is enough for a production eval?
My CSV import is losing settings — how do I fix it?
When should evals happen in the agent development lifecycle?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Unblocks the highest-value bottleneck in this portfolio (12 inbound requests/cycle, 11+ hrs absorbed). Force-multiplier: frees the eval SME from repetitive guidance loops while scaling eval quality and speed across the entire team and customer base.

candidateSkills.name: loop-pm-review-note-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates structured Loop meeting notes for PM reviews, product syncs, and stakeholder meetings. Auto-populates agenda sections, captures decisions and open questions, and formats action items with owners and due dates — using prior Loop docs and meeting context as input.
triggerExamples: Create Loop notes for today's CAPECAT PM review
Generate a meeting notes template for our weekly analytics sync
Prep the agenda and open questions doc before the PM review
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Saves 15-20 min per meeting note, standardizes output format across all PM reviews (addressing SGI risk), and ensures decisions and action items are never lost. Chain head — downstream action tracking and status reports depend on its quality.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Classifies inbound Azure DevOps email notifications by work item type (Task, Bug, Requirement, Epic, Feature) and routes or batches them into prioritized action groups with recommended next steps. Converts 22+ hours/week of passive email scanning into a structured, 15-minute triage session.
triggerExamples: Triage my ADO notifications from this week
Which ADO items actually need my attention today?
Batch my ADO emails by type and tell me what needs action
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Highest single time-recapture opportunity in the current portfolio (22.5 hrs/week). Deterministic classification eliminates passive inbox scanning. Natural decomposition point: sub-classifiers for IcM, Coverage, Evals, Tasks, and Epics.

candidateSkills.name: daily-briefing-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates a personalized daily planning page by pulling today's calendar events, flagged emails, priority tasks, and carry-forward items from yesterday. Replaces manual date-stamped daily prep documents with a context-aware, consistently structured briefing. First validated instance of Archetype 27: Personal Work Ritual.
triggerExamples: Help me prepare for my day
Generate my daily planning page for March 20
Give me a quick briefing before my first meeting
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Saves 15-20 min/day of manual planning = 60-80 hrs/year compounding. Highest daily-use skill candidate. Particularly valuable for individual contributors with fragmented calendars and high meeting loads. Builds an AI-assisted daily work habit loop.
